title: Japan PM arrives in Delhi; delighted to host you on your first visit to India, says Modi
content: This is Ms. Takaichi's first official visit to India. She is visiting at Mr. Modi's invitation from July 1 to 3 for the 16th India-Japan annual summit, the MEA earlier said.

title: Watch: 117 voices appeal for peace between India and Pakistan
content:
At least 117 prominent individuals from India and Pakistan have jointly appealed to Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and Shehbaz Sharif to revive bilateral dialogue and take steps towards lasting peace in the region.
In an open letter, they called for the resumption of discussions on Jammu and Kashmir, including revisiting the framework negotiated between 2004 and 2007. They also urged both countries to pursue demilitarisation, de-escalation and address each other’s legitimate security concerns.

title: The Hindu Home Expo at Kochi LuLu Mall on July 4, 5
content: More than 125 K-RERA-approved apartments and villas from 20 leading builders to be showcased; four banks to offer home loan schemes at preferential interest rates for visitors

title: NDA should retain power to ensure development of A.P., says CM Naidu
content: Accusing the YSRCP of trying incite conflict among different castes, religions, and regions, the Chief Minister urges people to ensure that the party does not return to power

title: Ayurveda targeting global markets; seeks govt support to maintain momentum
content: With India now targeting countries outside Europe, large private players in the sector have demanded sustained government backing

title: Manish Tewari left in the cold as Congress appoints new working presidents, poll panels for Punjab
content: State Congress chief Amarinder Singh Raja Warring keeps his job; former CM Charanjit Singh Channi to head campaign committee; three-term Lok Sabha member Manish Tewari is a notable omission

title: Safe footpath campaign in Bengaluru receives an unexpected Norwegian push
content: A group of senior officials from the Norwegian Embassy undertake a run down the footpaths of Bengaluru, shares a video extolling the experience

title: VB-GRAM G workers to get a minimum of ₹300 per day as wage rates notified by Centre
content: Highest wage rate of ₹409/day in Haryana; 21 States/UTs with lower wage rates under MGNREGA see significant hikes to meet new VB-GRAM G floor rate; southern States with higher wages see minimal hikes

title: Commercial LPG prices cut by ₹183 per refill
content: The first price cut this year follows a hike of ₹1,345 in four tranches; no respite for domestic LPG users; ATF prices reduced by ₹5 per litre; experts sound caution, say any flare-up in West Asia could hit supply chain

title: HSBC to set up integrated campus in Bengaluru
content: India is one of the most important talent centres in the world and continues to be a key market for HSBC: Georges Elhedery, group CEO, HSBC

title: SC panel urged to let disabled prisoners self-identify, declare their disabilities
content: The submissions recommend that prison records must identify every person with disability individually to make reasonable adjustments for them, while respecting their confidentiality

title: Ram Temple embezzlement case: 15 days extension for SIT probe
content: The U.P. government granted an extension to broaden the scope of the investigation and ensure that no culpable individual escapes justice, says senior official; next hearing in the case posted for July 13

title: Latest CRS data shows improving civil registration of births and deaths and better sex ratio at birth
content: While there is no surprise in the strong performance of Kerala (970 females per 1000 males) when it comes to sex ratio at birth, other States such as Arunachal Pradesh (1,050), Andaman and Nicobar Islands (984), Meghalaya (974), and Mizoram (972), are also top performers

title: THE HINDU IMPACT | Kerala HC initiates suo motu PIL into tree-felling on CHR land in Idukki
content: Allegations of large-scale tree-felling and unauthorised construction on 296 acres of cardamom title land on Gudanpara Estate, Idukki, falling under the Ponmudi Forest Section, Devikulam Range

title: Manipur burns again after pause
content: Kuki and Naga organisations accuse armed groups from each other’s communities of torching villages along the India-Myanmar border in Kamjong district

title: Kerala Governor gives assent to Seshadrinathan’s appointment as State Election Commissioner
content: The government had come under fire from within the Congress and also the Communist Party of India (Marxist) for nominating an alleged “Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) fellow traveller” to the post

title: TVK, post-poll partners move to institutionalise alliance with coordination committee, common minimum programme
content: Addressing a joint press conference after the meeting, Congress leader Manickam Tagore said it was the first round of discussions and all the parties had shared their views

title: A.P. CM Naidu lays stone for Hero MotoCorp’s Global Parts Centre in Tirupati district
content: Hero MotoCorp commits to ₹3200 crore investment in Andhra Pradesh to strengthen its global supply chain and reinforce India’s position in the automotive and EV ecosystem

title: Iran invites Congress president Kharge, former EAM Salman Khurshid, Pawan Khera for Khamenei funeral
content: The Indian delegation will be among a large group of foreign dignitaries that have been invited by Tehran for the burial-related events, which will start from July 3 and will continue till July 9

title: Practical restrictions on visitors from next Kerala Assembly session: Speaker Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan
content: Assembly had witnessed a huge influx of visitors during its first session after the Congress-led United Democratic Front government came to power in the State

title: Watch: Japan’s PM Takaichi in India: Indo-Pacific Ties in Focus | Above the Fold | 01.07.2026
content: This episode looks at the Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s first visit to India; key rule changes that come into effect from July 1, the Delhi High Court’s ruling in BJP MP Raghav Chadha’s case, the U.S. Supreme Court’s birthright citizenship ruling, and more.

title: Demography panel to study population changes post 2011 Census, prepares detailed questionnaire for States
content: The High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes will seek details of population changes and increase in settlements since the 2011 Census; it will also ask the ECI for data on SIR deletions

title: Air Marshal Jasvir Singh Mann takes charge as Air Officer Commanding-in-Chief of IAF’s Southern Air Command
content: Prior to the present appointment, Air Marshal Mann was Senior Air Staff Officer at the Western Air Command

title: CPI(M), CPI leaders meet Tamil Nadu CM Vijay, press various demands
content: The TVK-led Tamil Nadu government, before tabling a resolution in the Assembly on Mekedatu issue, should have called an all-party meeting to take the issue forward, said CPI(M) leader P. Shanmugam

title: Govt issues notice to WhatsApp over username feature
content: The Union government sent WhatsApp a notice about the feature over impersonation concerns. The feature was set to be rolled out in the coming months

title: Megha Engineering forms JV with Analog for Physical Intelligence solutions
content: Equal JV will invest $300-500 million over next three years, focus on addressing urban challenges such as traffic flow

title: Forest official injured in wild elephant attack in Periyar Tiger Reserve
content: The officer, along with his team, were returning from patrolling when they encountered a wild elephant

title: MSC-Adani Vizhinjam port deal: State govt. approval needed for share transfer
content: Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan says the proposed acquisition of stake in  AVPPL by MSC has come to the attention of the government through media reports. Adani port authorities had not initiated any correspondence or communication with the government in this regard

title: Minister assures remunerative prices to tobacco farmers
content: Inspecting a tobacco auction centre, Dola Sree Bala Veeranjaneya Swamy urges buyers to ensure that the purchase price of tobacco does not fall below ₹200 per kg

title: A.P. registers 16% growth in commercial taxes collection in June 2026
content: Net GST collections for June 2026 has also increased 21.35% year-on-year from ₹2,591 crore in June 2025 to ₹3,144 crore, says official

title: Birth tourism was never a problem: How Trump admin got humiliated in US Supreme Court
content: Following the Supreme Court's affirmation of birthright citizenship, President Trump plans to seek congressional action to limit it. A proposed bill by Republican lawmakers would require at least one parent to be a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident. While the administration cites birth tourism, government lawyers admitted to the Supreme Court that the practice's prevalence is unknown, with data suggesting it affects less than 1% of births annually.

title: Scientists create 'life': All about the SpudCell - why it matters & where it falls short
content: Scientists have engineered 'SpudCell,' a lab-made system from chemicals that mimics life's core functions like growth and division. While not fully alive due to reliance on external components, this breakthrough offers a simplified model to understand essential life processes. Researchers aim to enhance its self-sufficiency, paving the way for custom biological machines and deeper insights into fundamental biology.

title: 'Targeted act of vandalism': India condemns demolition of gurdwara in Pak
content: India has strongly condemned the reported demolition of a 125-year-old Sikh Gurdwara in Pakistan's Farooqabad, terming it a "targeted act of vandalism." The Ministry of External Affairs expressed grave concern over the lack of action by local authorities and the Evacuee Trust Property Board. This incident is seen as part of a continuing pattern of attacks on religious minorities and their places of worship in Pakistan.

title: Ukraine takes the war to Moscow: Why Kyiv unleashed its biggest attacks after G7 summit
content: Ukraine has launched its most extensive long-range drone campaign, targeting Russian oil refineries, fuel depots, and military infrastructure deep within its territory. This escalation follows G7 commitments to bolster Ukraine's air defenses and economic resilience. The strikes aim to disrupt Russia's war financing and logistics, with President Putin acknowledging temporary fuel shortages.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: From terror to tokens: Some of Trump's $1.4bn crypto jackpot comes via Pak
content: A 927-page disclosure filed with the US Office of Government Ethics shows Trump earned at least $2 billion in revenue during 2025, including an estimated $1.4 billion from cryptocurrency ventures controlled by trusts benefiting him. The extraordinary windfall has triggered fresh debate in Washington over conflicts of interest while simultaneously opening a geopolitical chapter involving Pakistan that could have adverse implications for India.

title: Govt directs Meta not to roll out WhatsApp username feature, seeks explanation; company reacts
content: India's government has halted WhatsApp's new username feature, demanding a detailed explanation within three days. Officials are concerned about impersonation, fraud, and weakened accountability, as users could be misled by similar-looking usernames. Cybersecurity experts and startup founders echo these fears, warning of potential scams and a decline in public trust.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: ‘Bas aaj ki raat hai zindagi’: Song Ketan danced to now carries painful irony
content: The singer, Reetu Sharma, who performed at the event on May 7, shared a poignant video that highlights the joyous atmosphere juxtaposed against the sorrow following Agarwal's purported murder on June 18.

title: Microsoft plans another round of layoffs, may cut thousands of jobs across teams in ...
content: Microsoft is gearing up for another round of job cuts, reportedly impacting sales, consulting, and Xbox divisions. While smaller than previous reductions, thousands of positions may be affected. This move comes as the tech giant prioritizes AI investments and cost control, even as its stock has seen a dip.  A voluntary retirement program earlier this year also aimed to manage headcount.

title: This was being called the 'new gold'. So what went wrong?
content: COMEX Silver has declined 37% since the US-Iran war and is down 52% from its all-time high. In domestic markets. MCX Silver has corrected 20% since the war and 46% from its all-time high.

title: EPF Scheme 2026 notified: From contributions to withdrawals - top things that change
content: EPF Scheme 2026: Although the overall provident fund framework remains largely unchanged, the revised EPF scheme introduces some changes that relate to contributions, benefits and compliance requirements.

title: China's new rocket launch tech could change the way we go to space
content: China is pioneering an innovative electromagnetic launch system, aiming to catapult rockets to supersonic speeds using electricity before engine ignition. This novel approach, unlike SpaceX's chemical rockets, could significantly reduce fuel needs and launch costs by leveraging ground-based power. Tests are reportedly progressing, with potential for a revolutionary shift in space access if successful.

title: Lions linked with Cowboys CB as Detroit eyes secondary reinforcements
content: The Detroit Lions could explore a trade with the Dallas Cowboys after releasing former first-round cornerback Terrion Arnold following his legal issues. Dallas has strong depth at cornerback, making Caelen Carson a potential trade candidate if the team stays healthy through training camp. While no deal is imminent, Carson's rookie contract and promising performances make him an affordable option for a Lions secondary that suddenly needs reinforcements before Week 1.

title: Chargers, Panthers emerge as favorites for Commanders star Daron Payne
content: Daron Payne's future with the Commanders hangs in the balance as he enters the final year of his substantial contract. With a new coaching staff and defensive scheme, his 2026 performance will be crucial. Several teams, including the Chargers, Jaguars, Panthers, and Lions, are reportedly monitoring his situation and could pursue him if he hits free agency next spring, seeking to bolster their defensive lines.

title: ExtraEmily banned on Twitch after near-crash during livestream
content: ExtraEmily has been banned from Twitch after a June 28 IRL stream showed her nearly crashing while reading chat during a drive. The incident revived concerns about distracted driving and followed previous controversies, including a viewbotting dispute and an earlier driving-related suspension. The latest ban has again sparked debate over safety rules for livestreamers and the responsibility creators have while broadcasting in public.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dubbed 'almost unplayable' as England receive major warning
content: England faces a significant challenge if teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debuts against them, according to former spinner Graeme Swann.  Swann described the young batter as "almost unplayable" and noted that few bowlers have found success against him.  While England's bowlers will be tested, Jofra Archer might possess an edge due to his IPL experience with Sooryavanshi.  The highly anticipated debut follows Sooryavanshi's stellar IPL 2026 season.

title: Russia turns to India for gasoline as fuel shortages deepen after Ukrainian strikes
content: The Kremlin said on Tuesday that it was in discussions with several countries about importing fuel at acceptable prices. One industry source said India has already shipped at least 60,000 metric tonnes of gasoline to Russia. Another source said two cargoes, each carrying between 30,000 and 40,000 tonnes, have been dispatched.

title: Iran invites Kharge, Khurshid, Khera to Khamenei's burial ceremony
content: Tehran had also invited Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend Khamenei's funeral. However, New Delhi chose Bihar governor Lt Gen Syed Ata Hasnain (Retd) and minister of state for external affairs Pabitra Margherita to represent India at the multi-day funeral, which is set to begin on July 4.

title: Big relief for Sreesanth! Kerala Cricket Association revokes three-year ban
content: Former India pacer S Sreesanth's three-year ban by the Kerala Cricket Association has been revoked. The decision follows Sreesanth's unconditional apology for his critical remarks against the association. This lifts the suspension, allowing him to continue as co-owner of the Aries Kollam Sailors franchise in the Kerala Cricket League. The KCA warned of strict action for any future misconduct.

title: 'Give statutory status to NTA': House panel suggests 'NEET in multiple phases' amid paper leak row
content: A Parliamentary panel has recommended conducting NEET-UG in multiple phases across states to ease logistical pressures and suggested granting statutory status to the NTA for stronger examination oversight.  Officials briefed the committee on the NEET re-examination's success and discussed reforms. Concerns were raised about a potential shift to Computer-Based Tests and latecomers being denied entry.

title: India-Bangladesh cricket ties back on track? BCB's media rights tender sparks hope
content: India and Bangladesh are poised to resume cricketing ties with a six-match white-ball series in September. The Bangladesh Cricket Board has initiated the process by floating a tender for media rights, signaling a potential thaw in relations after a year of strain. This development follows earlier postponements and diplomatic complexities, with the series tentatively scheduled to begin in early September.

title: Hormuz oil shock sends India back to Russia: Is this a peak or the new normal?
content: Russia has emerged as the dominant supplier of crude oil for India - with imports touching over 240 million barrels in the four months since the US-Iran conflict began. In fact, crude imports from Russia are over 4 times the nearest supplier - UAE at over 58 million barrels.

title: India, Japan to sign LNG security pact during PM Modi-Takaichi bilateral
content: India and Japan are set to sign a crucial agreement to bolster liquefied natural gas (LNG) supply security, aiming to counter global disruptions. A joint task force is anticipated to coordinate stockpiling and enhance energy cooperation. The summit, focusing on economic security and resilient supply chains, will also cover critical minerals, AI, and semiconductors, with significant private sector investments expected.

title: 100+ eminent Indians, Pakistanis urge PM Modi, Sharif to revive bilateral dialogue
content: Among the signatories are former RAW chief A S Dulat, Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha, moderate Hurriyat leader Mirwaiz Umar Farooq, former Union minister Mani Shankar Aiyar, former Pakistan foreign minister Khurshid Mahmud Kasuri, former diplomat Ashraf Jehangir Qazi, along with retired diplomats and members of civil society from both countries.

title: 'Everybody's profiting': Trump reacts to $1.4bn income disclosure
content: US President Trump reported over $1.4 billion in income from cryptocurrency ventures, including his family's World Liberty Financial and a $TRUMP meme coin. He stated he doesn't manage personal finances, attributing profits to the strong stock market. Despite ethics concerns over crypto-friendly policies enacted during his term, the White House denied any conflicts of interest, asserting Trump made the US the 'crypto capital'.

title: Akanksha Chamola vs Ram Kapoor: Is cheating really a ‘mistake’ or a ‘choice’?
content: Akanksha Chamola's strong stance against infidelity on Lock Upp Season 2 ignited a debate: is cheating a mistake or a choice? Experts and psychological research suggest it's a conscious decision, not an accident. Studies reveal powerful individuals may be more prone to affairs, often with little regret, highlighting a complex reality beyond impulsive actions.

title: 'I was not utilised properly': Shardul's explosive claim on Gill-led Eng tour
content: India all-rounder Shardul Thakur expressed his disappointment over being "underutilized" during last year's Test series in England.  He believes he could have made a significant impact had he been given more bowling opportunities, citing a "calculation error" in how he was used. Despite the missed chances, Thakur remains hopeful for future national team call-ups, emphasizing his readiness to contribute.

title: Bengaluru daycare horror: Toddlers put in washing machine, sprayed with toilet jet
content: Five women daycare staff at an IT company campus in Bengaluru face an FIR for allegedly abusing toddlers. Disturbing videos surfaced, showing caregivers threatening and physically mistreating children aged two to three. Allegations include locking children in bathrooms and using a washing machine. Police are questioning the accused and verifying the footage to determine the extent of the abuse.

title: China tightens curbs on foreign investments amid AI, chip tech rivalry with US
content: China has unveiled stringent new regulations to bolster oversight of its overseas investments, particularly in critical tech sectors like AI and semiconductors. These rules, effective Wednesday, aim to safeguard national security and technological capabilities amidst escalating US competition. While designed to enhance outward investment quality, they could complicate global expansion for Chinese firms and access to Chinese capital for foreign partners, raising concerns about technology transfers and broader geopolitical implications.

title: Haaland scores, then dons cowboy gear before unleashing his inner Viking
content: Norway's star striker Erling Haaland celebrated his team's FIFA World Cup Round of 16 qualification with a series of fun social media posts, including cowboy and Viking themes.  His dramatic 86th-minute winner against Ivory Coast secured their spot, making him the fastest to 60 international goals for Norway.  They now face Brazil in the next round.

title: Govt warns of strict action amid fraud concerns over WhatsApp usernames
content: Authorities have issued a stern warning to WhatsApp regarding its new username feature, emphasizing that the platform will be held legally accountable for any cyber fraud or impersonation facilitated by the update. The government is scrutinizing the feature, which could allow users to adopt names similar to official entities, raising significant concerns about potential misuse. WhatsApp, however, defends the update as a privacy enhancement, claiming built-in safeguards against abuse.

title: Will Bronny James leave Los Angeles Lakers? Here’s what to know following LeBron James’ exit
content: LeBron James' decision to leave the Los Angeles Lakers has sparked fresh questions about Bronny James' future, but current signs point toward the young guard remaining with the franchise. Bronny's fully guaranteed contract, steady development over his first two NBA seasons and affordable salary make him a valuable depth option as the Lakers transition into a new era built around Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves. While speculation persists about a potential reunion with LeBron on his next team, Los Angeles is under no obligation to facilitate a move, leaving Bronny well positioned to continue carving out his own NBA career.

title: Degrees of denial: Paris blames US for climate change in heated exchange
content: A transatlantic debate ignites as Paris blames America's heavy air conditioning use for contributing to Europe's heatwave deaths. While the US mocks France's lack of AC, experts highlight America's significant emissions. The article contrasts this with the US's own struggles with extreme heat and a dismissive attitude towards climate science, underscoring the urgent need for sustainable cooling solutions.

title: 'We are starting from zero again': Shreyas Iyer reveals Gambhir's message
content: Shreyas Iyer embraces his new role as India's T20I captain, viewing it as a childhood dream realized. Despite a tough start in Ireland, Iyer is focused on instilling a fearless, aggressive brand of cricket. He aims to build a fresh team identity, learning from past experiences and the support staff to forge a relentless and determined unit ready for the challenges ahead.

title: Siya, Chetan 'rehearsed' Ketan's murder on another hill before executing it at Lohagad Fort
content: In a shocking turn of events, Pune Rural police revealed that realtor Ketan Agarwal's fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly rehearsed his murder before pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort. The couple confessed to practicing the act on a hill near Market Yard. Police are planning a scene reconstruction and gait analysis of Chaudhary.

title: 'Carrying water is not degrading': Ashwin addresses criticism over Sooryavanshi
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has defended his stance on young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's role, emphasizing that carrying drinks and observing from the bench offers valuable learning.  He clarified his earlier comments, stating that such tasks are not demeaning and contribute to a player's understanding of team dynamics.  Despite a stellar IPL, Sooryavanshi's debut is delayed, with Ashwin suggesting patience for the 15-year-old's promising career.

title: 25 Years After CNG Revolution, Delhi's Auto Drivers Face A New Transition
content: As Delhi prepares for its biggest electric mobility push since the CNG revolution, auto drivers who lived through hours-long queues and fuel shortages say the city has one chance to avoid repeating history: build the infrastructure before the transition gathers speed.

title: AI Emerges As Top Cyber Threat for Next 12 Months, Companies Underprepared: Report
content: Rapid advances in AI can increase the sophistication, speed and scale of cyber incidents, the RBI said in its latest Financial Stability Report.

title: German Prosecutors Charge Suspect In Nord Stream Pipeline Sabotage
content: Federal prosecutors confirmed that a man had been charged over the explosion, with German media reporting that he was a Ukrainian national said to be the head of the team that carried out the operation.

title: High-Profile Names Withheld: WhatsApp To NDTV On Impersonation Concern
content: A WhatsApp spokesperson said the really high-profile names -- public figures, government entities and celebrities -- have been withheld so they can be claimed by legitimate owners.

title: A Peek Inside Trump's $400 Million Qatar-Gifted Air Force One On Debut Trip
content: Reporters are generally not permitted to take photos on the plane unless US President Donald Trump is present. But on Wednesday, Trump administration staffers posted images of the plane's interior on social media.

title: Defence Panel To Consider Proposals Worth Over Rs 1 Lakh Crore On Friday
content: Chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, the panel is expected to consider granting Acceptance of Necessity (AoN) to several high-value acquisition proposals aimed at strengthening the operational capabilities of the armed forces.

title: Toddlers Kept In Washing Machines, Locked In Bathrooms: Horror At Bengaluru Daycare
content: A case has been filed against five women caregivers at the centre, which was operating inside the HAL campus of Capgemini.

title: Karnataka Teen Decapitated After Leaning Out Of Moving Bus Window
content: According to police, Pakkaresh was travelling on a bus from Hubballi to Lakshmeshwar and was seated near the rear of the vehicle.

title: "Everybody's Profiting": Trump Defends Earning More Than $1 Billion On Crypto
content: Asked about criticisms that he was using his position to enrich himself, the billionaire president said his earnings were placed in blind trusts to ensure that he could not do so.

title: Centre Asks Meta Not To Roll Out WhatsApp IDs Until Consultation: Sources
content: The "username" feature will allow users to pick a username and connect without sharing their phone numbers.

title: Why These Cities And Shrines Were Chosen For Ali Khamenei's Funeral Journey
content: From the seat of power in Tehran to the Shia holy cities of Qom, Karbala, Najaf and finally Mashhad, each stop holds symbolic importance.

title: Rs 1 Lakh To 20 Lakh: Who Looted How Much In Ram Temple Theft Case
content: According to police documents prepared at the time of his arrest, Avinash Shukla, who stole the largest amount, confirmed that the theft occurred during the counting of the offerings

title: India's Leading Position In Global Remittance Market Driven By US, UAE
content: Remittances are recognised as one of the most stable and dependable components of external financing. They remain remarkably well-insulated from broader financial market volatility and geopolitical uncertainty.

title: No Names, No Faces, No Phones: Secret Lives Of Ukraine's Shadow Drone Unit
content: The retaliatory attacks on Russian military sites and oil refineries they carry out have embarrassed President Vladimir Putin, who four years ago thought he could capture Kyiv in a matter of days.

title: Who Really Runs Iran Now, After Ali Khamenei's Death
content: US President Donald Trump said last month that the war had removed a "first set" and "second set" of leaders but maintained that the "third set" was "smart", "very rational" and "not radicalised".

title: Israel Says Troops Will Stay In Lebanon, Syria, Gaza "Until Further Notice"
content: Israel and Lebanon signed a US-sponsored framework agreement on Friday to pave the way for peace between the two countries and disarm Iran-backed group Hezbollah.

title: Russia Turns To India For Gasoline Amid Fuel Shortages: Report
content: Gasoline consumption in Russia is at least 110,000 tons per day in summer, when demand for fuel is high.

title: Electric Shocks, 18-Hour Labour: 25 Indians Forced Into Cybercrime In Myanmar
content: Kaustubh Shejwal, a 30-year-old resident of Mangalmurti Nagar in the suburbs of Nashik, has been held captive in Myanmar since April. His family is now running from pillar to post to secure the release of the captives.

title: "Very Good Meetings": Trump On US-Iran Talks In Doha
content: The US leader said there had been "very good meetings" in Doha after an early dispute over the format threatened to overshadow discussions.
